questions.stem: The famous novel Tom Sawyer _____ by Mark Twain in 1876.
questions.explanation: 选C。本题考查一般过去时的被动语态。书是被写的，所以要用被动语态be+过去分词；1876年是过去时间，所以用was written。A是主动语态第三人称单数；B是主动语态过去式；D是现在时被动语态，时态不对。解题技巧：看到'by+人'的结构，首先考虑被动语态；再看时间状语判断时态。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—_____ you _____ the film Titanic?
—Yes, I saw it three years ago.
questions.explanation: 选B。问句询问是否看过这部电影（强调对现在的影响，即是否了解这部电影内容），所以用现在完成时Have you seen；答句中的three years ago是具体的过去时间状语，所以用一般过去时saw。A选项Did you see虽然语法正确，但强调过去的动作而非现在的影响；C是一般现在时；D是将来时。解题技巧：现在完成时强调'经历/影响'，一般过去时强调'过去某个具体时间发生的动作'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The girl _____ is wearing a red dress is my cousin. She just came back from Canada.
questions.explanation: 选B。本题考查定语从句关系词的选择。先行词是the girl（人），且关系词在从句中作主语（is wearing），所以用who。which指物；whose表示'谁的'，在从句中作定语；whom指人但只能作宾语。解题技巧：指人用who/whom/that，指物用which/that；从句缺主语用who/which，缺宾语可用whom/who/which。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Is that Mr. Wang knocking at the door?
—It _____ be him. He has gone to Shanghai on business.
questions.explanation: 选C。本题考查情态动词表推测。根据后句'He has gone to Shanghai'（他去上海出差了，现在还没回来），可以100%确定门外的人不可能是他，所以用can't表示'不可能'。must be表示'一定是'（肯定推测）；may/might be表示'可能是'（不确定推测）。解题技巧：情态动词表推测时，can't用于否定句表示'不可能'；must用于肯定句表示'一定'；may/might表示'可能'。看到'has gone to'（去了某地未回）要立刻想到是否定推测。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
The weather was terrible yesterday. It was raining heavily and the wind was blowing strongly. People could hardly go outside.
What does the underlined word 'terrible' mean?
questions.explanation: 选B。根据上下文线索'raining heavily'（雨下得很大）和'wind was blowing strongly'（风刮得很大）以及'could hardly go outside'（几乎不能外出），可以推断terrible是'糟糕的、恶劣的'意思。A意思相反；C和D只是描述温度，而原文描述的是风雨交加的综合恶劣天气。解题技巧：猜词题要关注前后的同义词、反义词或解释说明性的短语。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: We should _____ the lights when we leave the classroom to save electricity. It's a good habit.
questions.explanation: 选B。turn off表示'关闭'（电器、水源等），符合句意'离开教室时关灯省电'。turn on是'打开'，与句意相反；turn up是'调大（音量）'或'出现'；turn down是'调小（音量）'或'拒绝'。解题技巧：turn短语是中考高频考点，记住：on/off表示开关，up/down表示大小/高低。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: It is _____ to play with fire. You may hurt yourself or others.
questions.explanation: 选B。It is + adj. + to do sth.是固定句型，表示'做某事是……的'，所以此处需要形容词dangerous。A是名词；C是副词；D是动词（危害）。解题技巧：记住句型It is + 形容词 + (for sb.) to do sth.，看到it is...to结构，中间通常填形容词。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.explanation: 选A。根据下文'However, after seeing some examples online, they changed their minds'（然而，看了网上的例子后，他们改变了主意），可知一开始学生们觉得这个项目'无聊/没意思'，所以不想参加。boring表示'无聊的'；interesting（有趣的）与句意相反；important（重要的）和necessary（必要的）不符合语境转折逻辑。解题技巧：完形填空要关注转折词（however/but），前后语义通常相反。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project called "Waste to Art". Our teacher, Mr. Li, asked us to collect recyclable materials and turn them into useful items or artworks.

At first, some students thought it was (8)_____ and didn't want to join. They said they were too busy with their studies. However, after seeing some examples online, they changed their minds.

My group decided to make a bookshelf from old cardboard boxes. We (9)_____ three days designing and building it. The process was not easy because we had to make sure it was strong enough to hold heavy books. We also painted it with bright colors to make it look (10)_____.

When we finished, we put the bookshelf in our classroom library. Everyone was surprised by how professional it looked. Mr. Li praised us, saying that our work was (11)_____ than he had expected.

This project taught us an important lesson: if we use our creativity, we can (12)_____ waste into treasure. Now, more students want to join the environmental club to learn more about recycling.

questions.explanation: 选A。spend time (in) doing sth.是固定搭配，表示'花费时间做某事'，主语是人。take通常用it takes sb. time to do sth.句型；cost主语是物，指花费金钱；pay通常指支付金钱，搭配for。此处主语是we，后面是designing，所以用spent。解题技巧：'人+spend+时间/金钱+on sth./(in) doing sth.'；'It takes+人+时间+to do sth.'；'物+cost+人+金钱'。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project called "Waste to Art". Our teacher, Mr. Li, asked us to collect recyclable materials and turn them into useful items or artworks.

At first, some students thought it was (8)_____ and didn't want to join. They said they were too busy with their studies. However, after seeing some examples online, they changed their minds.

My group decided to make a bookshelf from old cardboard boxes. We (9)_____ three days designing and building it. The process was not easy because we had to make sure it was strong enough to hold heavy books. We also painted it with bright colors to make it look (10)_____.

When we finished, we put the bookshelf in our classroom library. Everyone was surprised by how professional it looked. Mr. Li praised us, saying that our work was (11)_____ than he had expected.

This project taught us an important lesson: if we use our creativity, we can (12)_____ waste into treasure. Now, more students want to join the environmental club to learn more about recycling.

questions.explanation: 选C。根据上文'painted it with bright colors'（涂上了鲜艳的颜色），目的是让书架看起来'有吸引力的、漂亮的'。attractive表示'吸引人的'；big（大的）和heavy（重的）与涂颜色无关；expensive（昂贵的）不符合语境，因为这是用废旧材料做的。解题技巧：根据动作（painted bright colors）推断目的（更好看/更吸引人）。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project called "Waste to Art". Our teacher, Mr. Li, asked us to collect recyclable materials and turn them into useful items or artworks.

At first, some students thought it was (8)_____ and didn't want to join. They said they were too busy with their studies. However, after seeing some examples online, they changed their minds.

My group decided to make a bookshelf from old cardboard boxes. We (9)_____ three days designing and building it. The process was not easy because we had to make sure it was strong enough to hold heavy books. We also painted it with bright colors to make it look (10)_____.

When we finished, we put the bookshelf in our classroom library. Everyone was surprised by how professional it looked. Mr. Li praised us, saying that our work was (11)_____ than he had expected.

This project taught us an important lesson: if we use our creativity, we can (12)_____ waste into treasure. Now, more students want to join the environmental club to learn more about recycling.

questions.explanation: 选B。后面有than，提示要用比较级。better than expected是固定表达，表示'比预期的更好'，符合老师表扬学生的语境。good是原级；best是最高级；worse（更差）与praised（表扬）矛盾。解题技巧：看到than就选比较级，看到of/in范围选最高级。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project called "Waste to Art". Our teacher, Mr. Li, asked us to collect recyclable materials and turn them into useful items or artworks.

At first, some students thought it was (8)_____ and didn't want to join. They said they were too busy with their studies. However, after seeing some examples online, they changed their minds.

My group decided to make a bookshelf from old cardboard boxes. We (9)_____ three days designing and building it. The process was not easy because we had to make sure it was strong enough to hold heavy books. We also painted it with bright colors to make it look (10)_____.

When we finished, we put the bookshelf in our classroom library. Everyone was surprised by how professional it looked. Mr. Li praised us, saying that our work was (11)_____ than he had expected.

This project taught us an important lesson: if we use our creativity, we can (12)_____ waste into treasure. Now, more students want to join the environmental club to learn more about recycling.

questions.explanation: 选A。turn...into...是固定搭配，表示'把……变成……'，句意为'把废品变成财富'。take into（考虑到；带入）；bring into（带入）；carry into（搬进）都没有'转变'的含义。解题技巧：turn A into B = change A into B，记住这个环保主题常用搭配。
questions.label: 完形填空
questions.group_passage:
Last month, our class started a special project called "Waste to Art". Our teacher, Mr. Li, asked us to collect recyclable materials and turn them into useful items or artworks.

questions.stem: When did Narayana Peesapaty start his company Bakeys?
questions.explanation: 选B。根据文章第二段第一句'In 2010, he started a company called Bakeys to make edible spoons.'可知，他是在2010年创办的公司。A、D选项时间错误；C选项2016年是他发布视频获得关注的时间。解题技巧：细节理解题要定位关键词（人名、年份、数字），在原文中找到完全对应的句子。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery is one of the biggest polluters of our oceans? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks, and knives are thrown away after just one use. They take hundreds of years to break down.

Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wanted to solve this problem. In 2010, he started a company called Bakeys to make edible spoons. These spoons are made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱). They are hard enough to eat ice cream or stir hot coffee, but you can also eat them when you finish your meal.

Peesapaty got the idea when he saw people throwing plastic spoons out of train windows during a business trip. "I thought, why not make spoons that you can eat?" he said. "If you don't eat them, they will naturally decompose (分解) in about five days."

At first, it was difficult to find money for his project. Many people didn't believe his idea would work. However, after he posted a video online in 2016, people from all over the world became interested. They donated money to help him build a factory.

Today, Bakeys produces thousands of spoons every day. They come in different flavors like sugar, ginger-garlic, and cumin (孜然). Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones and help save our planet.

questions.stem: Why was it difficult for Peesapaty to find money at the beginning?
options: A. Because he didn't have a good business plan.
B. Because many people didn't believe his idea would work.
C. Because the spoons were too expensive to make.
D. Because he didn't know how to use the Internet.
questions.explanation: 选B。根据第四段'Many people didn't believe his idea would work.'可知，一开始很多人不相信他的想法可行，所以很难筹到资金。A选项文中未提及；C选项是结果而非原因；D选项与后文他在网上发布视频不符。解题技巧：推理题要基于原文信息，'didn't believe his idea would work'直接导致'difficult to find money'，这是因果关系。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery is one of the biggest polluters of our oceans? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks, and knives are thrown away after just one use. They take hundreds of years to break down.

Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wanted to solve this problem. In 2010, he started a company called Bakeys to make edible spoons. These spoons are made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱). They are hard enough to eat ice cream or stir hot coffee, but you can also eat them when you finish your meal.

Peesapaty got the idea when he saw people throwing plastic spoons out of train windows during a business trip. "I thought, why not make spoons that you can eat?" he said. "If you don't eat them, they will naturally decompose (分解) in about five days."

At first, it was difficult to find money for his project. Many people didn't believe his idea would work. However, after he posted a video online in 2016, people from all over the world became interested. They donated money to help him build a factory.

Today, Bakeys produces thousands of spoons every day. They come in different flavors like sugar, ginger-garlic, and cumin (孜然). Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones and help save our planet.

questions.stem:
What does the underlined word 'replace' mean in the last paragraph?
'Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones and help save our planet.'
questions.explanation: 选A。根据上下文，Peesapaty发明可食用勺子是为了解决塑料污染问题，所以他希望可食用餐具能'取代'塑料餐具。replace = take the place of（代替）。B（和……一起玩）、C（看起来像）、D（和……一起工作）都不符合语境。解题技巧：词义猜测题看上下文逻辑，可食用餐具vs塑料餐具是对立关系，目标是替代以减少污染。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery is one of the biggest polluters of our oceans? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks, and knives are thrown away after just one use. They take hundreds of years to break down.

Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wanted to solve this problem. In 2010, he started a company called Bakeys to make edible spoons. These spoons are made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱). They are hard enough to eat ice cream or stir hot coffee, but you can also eat them when you finish your meal.

Peesapaty got the idea when he saw people throwing plastic spoons out of train windows during a business trip. "I thought, why not make spoons that you can eat?" he said. "If you don't eat them, they will naturally decompose (分解) in about five days."

At first, it was difficult to find money for his project. Many people didn't believe his idea would work. However, after he posted a video online in 2016, people from all over the world became interested. They donated money to help him build a factory.

Today, Bakeys produces thousands of spoons every day. They come in different flavors like sugar, ginger-garlic, and cumin (孜然). Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones and help save our planet.

questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The Problem of Plastic Pollution
B. How to Make Edible Spoons
C. The Man Who Made Edible Spoons
questions.explanation: 选C。本文主要讲述了印度科学家Narayana Peesapaty发明可食用勺子的故事，包括他的灵感来源、遇到的困难以及现在的成就。C选项'制作可食用勺子的男人'最能概括全文主旨。A选项（塑料污染问题）只是背景；B选项（如何制作可食用勺子）文中没有详细介绍制作过程；D选项（勺子的不同口味）只是细节。解题技巧：主旨大意题要涵盖全文核心内容，通常是'谁做了什么'，而不是某个细节或背景。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意
questions.group_passage:
Do you know that plastic cutlery is one of the biggest polluters of our oceans? Every year, millions of plastic spoons, forks, and knives are thrown away after just one use. They take hundreds of years to break down.

Narayana Peesapaty, a scientist from India, wanted to solve this problem. In 2010, he started a company called Bakeys to make edible spoons. These spoons are made from rice, wheat, and sorghum (高粱). They are hard enough to eat ice cream or stir hot coffee, but you can also eat them when you finish your meal.

Peesapaty got the idea when he saw people throwing plastic spoons out of train windows during a business trip. "I thought, why not make spoons that you can eat?" he said. "If you don't eat them, they will naturally decompose (分解) in about five days."

At first, it was difficult to find money for his project. Many people didn't believe his idea would work. However, after he posted a video online in 2016, people from all over the world became interested. They donated money to help him build a factory.

Today, Bakeys produces thousands of spoons every day. They come in different flavors like sugar, ginger-garlic, and cumin (孜然). Peesapaty hopes that one day, edible cutlery will replace plastic ones and help save our planet.

options: A. This is because your brain needs time to process information.
B. However, with the right methods, you can improve your memory easily.
C. In addition, try to connect new knowledge with what you already know.
D. As a result, your memory will become worse if you don't sleep well.
E. Then, organize your notes in a clear way.
questions.explanation: 选B。前一句说'很多学生抱怨他们很快忘记学过的东西'，后一句说'这里有一些有用的建议'，中间需要转折过渡，表示'但是，用正确的方法，你可以轻松提高记忆力'。However表示转折，承接上文的抱怨并引出下文的解决方案。其他选项：A解释原因，但前文是抱怨而非结论；C是递进；D是结果；E是顺承，都不符合此处转折逻辑。解题技巧：选句子填空要关注连接词，抱怨→转折→解决方案是常见结构。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they forget what they have learned quickly. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips to help you.

First, review your notes regularly. Don't wait until the night before the exam. (18)_____ This helps move information from short-term memory to long-term memory.

Second, use colorful pens when taking notes. Different colors can help your brain remember things better. (19)_____ For example, use red for important dates and blue for new vocabulary.

Finally, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to study, but this actually hurts memory. (20)_____ So make sure you sleep at least 8 hours every night.

options: A. This is because your brain needs time to process information.
B. However, with the right methods, you can improve your memory easily.
C. In addition, try to connect new knowledge with what you already know.
D. As a result, your memory will become worse if you don't sleep well.
E. Then, organize your notes in a clear way.
questions.explanation: 选A。前一句说'不要等到考试前一晚才复习'，后一句说'这有助于将信息从短期记忆转移到长期记忆'，中间需要解释'为什么不要临时抱佛脚'——因为大脑需要时间处理信息。This is because...解释了前文建议的原因。其他选项：B是转折（已用过）；C是递进；D是结果（与sleep相关，但此处讲复习）；E是顺承，但organize与review regularly逻辑不紧密。解题技巧：This is because...通常用于解释前文的原因。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they forget what they have learned quickly. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips to help you.

First, review your notes regularly. Don't wait until the night before the exam. (18)_____ This helps move information from short-term memory to long-term memory.

Second, use colorful pens when taking notes. Different colors can help your brain remember things better. (19)_____ For example, use red for important dates and blue for new vocabulary.

Finally, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to study, but this actually hurts memory. (20)_____ So make sure you sleep at least 8 hours every night.

options: A. This is because your brain needs time to process information.
B. However, with the right methods, you can improve your memory easily.
C. In addition, try to connect new knowledge with what you already know.
D. As a result, your memory will become worse if you don't sleep well.
E. Then, organize your notes in a clear way.
questions.explanation: 选E。本段主题是记笔记的方法（use colorful pens），前一句讲用不同颜色，后一句举例'用红色记日期，蓝色记单词'，中间需要另一个关于笔记整理的建议。Then表示顺承，'然后，以清晰的方式组织你的笔记'，与后文的例子（颜色分类）完美衔接。其他选项：A是原因解释；B是转折；C是另一条建议但连接词In addition不如Then顺承；D与睡眠相关。解题技巧：Then用于顺序列举，看到For example通常前面是总述句。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they forget what they have learned quickly. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips to help you.

First, review your notes regularly. Don't wait until the night before the exam. (18)_____ This helps move information from short-term memory to long-term memory.

Second, use colorful pens when taking notes. Different colors can help your brain remember things better. (19)_____ For example, use red for important dates and blue for new vocabulary.

Finally, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to study, but this actually hurts memory. (20)_____ So make sure you sleep at least 8 hours every night.

options: A. This is because your brain needs time to process information.
B. However, with the right methods, you can improve your memory easily.
C. In addition, try to connect new knowledge with what you already know.
D. As a result, your memory will become worse if you don't sleep well.
E. Then, organize your notes in a clear way.
questions.explanation: 选D。本段讲睡眠与记忆的关系，前一句说'熬夜学习实际上损害记忆力'，后一句说'所以确保每晚至少睡8小时'，中间需要结果句'因此，如果你不睡好，记忆力会变差'。As a result表示结果，承接前文的hurts memory。其他选项：A是原因；B是转折；C是递进；E是顺序。解题技巧：看到So（所以）在句首，前面通常有As a result/Therefore等表结果的句子。
questions.label: 短文填空
questions.group_passage:
Many students complain that they forget what they have learned quickly. (17)_____ Here are some useful tips to help you.

First, review your notes regularly. Don't wait until the night before the exam. (18)_____ This helps move information from short-term memory to long-term memory.

Second, use colorful pens when taking notes. Different colors can help your brain remember things better. (19)_____ For example, use red for important dates and blue for new vocabulary.

Finally, get enough sleep. Many students stay up late to study, but this actually hurts memory. (20)_____ So make sure you sleep at least 8 hours every night.
